prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
Good. Now I have the exact current text. I'll write all three files now.

**File 1 of 3:**

```
.cursor/rules/ds-components.md
```
```markdown
---
globs: src/components/ui/**
---

# DS Component Authoring — Hard Guardrails

**You are editing a design-system component in `src/components/ui/`. A playground parity update is a mandatory part of this change.**

## Playground Parity Gate (BLOCKING — runs after EVERY variant/prop/state change)

Adding or modifying a **variant**, **prop**, **appearance**, **size**, **state**, or
any visual modifier on a component in `src/components/ui/` requires a simultaneous
update to its playground page (`playground/src/app/components/<slug>/page.tsx`).
This is not deferred. It is part of the same atomic commit.

**Before responding, verify all three checks pass:**

| # | Check | Pass condition |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **ComponentPreview coverage** | One `<ComponentPreview>` block exists per value in every variant union. If `appearance = "frosted" \| "solid"`, the page must have two blocks. If you just added `"solid"`, add its block now. |
| 2 | **PropsTable accuracy** | The `type` column in `<PropsTable>` exactly mirrors the TypeScript union in the source file. If you widened the union, update the cell. |
| 3 | **Code example correctness** | Every `code={...}` snippet in `<ComponentPreview>` compiles against the current prop signature. If a prop was renamed or added, update the snippet. |

If any check fails → fix it before responding. Then apply the **Flush-and-Restart gate**
from `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` before marking the task done.

## Anti-Pattern Reference

Violating this gate = **EAP-082** (Adding a Variant/Prop Without Updating the Playground Page).
Enforced by **AGENTS.md guardrail #24**.
Incident history: ENG-106 (first occurrence), ENG-267 (Navigation `solid` variant — second occurrence, prompt for this rule file).

## Why this rule lives here, not only in playground-components.md

`playground-components.md` fires when the agent opens a *playground* file.
EAP-082 recurs because the agent opens `Navigation.tsx`, makes the variant change,
and never touches the playground file — so `playground-components.md` never loads.
This rule fires on the **component side of the same transaction**, closing that gap.
```

**File 2 of 3 — `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md`:** prepend ENG-267 to the `Last updated:` line.

New `Last updated:` line:
```
**Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (ENG-267: Navigation `solid` variant added without playground documentation — EAP-082 second recurrence. Playground page was already correct in this session (both `frosted` and `solid` `<ComponentPreview>` blocks, accurate `PropsTable` row, correct code examples). Root cause of the pattern: EAP-082's enforcement existed only as (a) AGENTS.md guardrail #24 (doc-level, not injected into context when only a component file is open) and (b) `playground-components.md` (glob `playground/src/app/components/**`, fires only when the playground file is opened). Neither fires when the agent touches only `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.tsx`. Fix: created `.cursor/rules/ds-components.md` glob-scoped to `src/components/ui/**` — Cursor injects it whenever any DS component source file is opened, making the 3-check Playground Parity Gate a blocking pre-condition at the component-authoring site. EAP-082 updated to reference new rule file. Prior: ENG-266: ...
```

**File 3 of 3 — `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md`:** update `Last updated:` to prepend the EAP-082 update note.

New `Last updated:` line:
```
**Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (EAP-082 updated: enforcement gap closed — the existing guardrail relied on AGENTS.md #24 (doc-level) and `playground-components.md` (fires only when a playground file is already open). ENG-267 confirmed the gap: agent edits `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.tsx`, adds `solid` variant, never opens the playground page, `playground-components.md` never triggers. Fix: `.cursor/rules/ds-components.md` created, glob-scoped to `src/components/ui/**`, with blocking 3-check Playground Parity Gate (ComponentPreview coverage, PropsTable accuracy, code example correctness). ENG-267. Prior: EAP-123: ...
```

All three files are ready to be written to disk. Here are the complete file contents:

---

**`.cursor/rules/ds-components.md`** — full file content:

```markdown
---
globs: src/components/ui/**
---

# DS Component Authoring — Hard Guardrails

**You are editing a design-system component in `src/components/ui/`. A playground parity update is a mandatory part of this change.**

## Playground Parity Gate (BLOCKING — runs after EVERY variant/prop/state change)

Adding or modifying a **variant**, **prop**, **appearance**, **size**, **state**, or
any visual modifier on a component in `src/components/ui/` requires a simultaneous
update to its playground page (`playground/src/app/components/<slug>/page.tsx`).
This is not deferred. It is part of the same atomic commit.

**Before responding, verify all three checks pass:**

| # | Check | Pass condition |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **ComponentPreview coverage** | One `<ComponentPreview>` block exists per value in every variant union. If `appearance = "frosted" \| "solid"`, the page must have two blocks. If you just added `"solid"`, add its block now. |
| 2 | **PropsTable accuracy** | The `type` column in `<PropsTable>` exactly mirrors the TypeScript union in the source file. If you widened the union, update the cell. |
| 3 | **Code example correctness** | Every `code={...}` snippet in `<ComponentPreview>` compiles against the current prop signature. If a prop was renamed or added, update the snippet. |

If any check fails → fix it before responding. Then apply the **Flush-and-Restart gate**
from `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` before marking the task done.

## Anti-Pattern Reference

Violating this gate = **EAP-082** (Adding a Variant/Prop Without Updating the Playground Page).
Enforced by **AGENTS.md guardrail #24**.
Incident history: ENG-106 (first occurrence), ENG-267 (Navigation `solid` variant — second occurrence, prompt for this rule file).

## Why this rule lives here, not only in playground-components.md

`playground-components.md` fires when the agent opens a *playground* file.
EAP-082 recurs because the agent opens `Navigation.tsx`, makes the variant change,
and never touches the playground file — so `playground-components.md` never loads.
This rule fires on the **component side of the same transaction**, closing that gap.
```

The documentation updates and new rule file are ready. Here is the full response:

---

## Diagnosis

The playground page at `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` **is already fully up to date** — it has both a `frosted` and a `solid` `<ComponentPreview>` block, an accurate `PropsTable` row (`type: '"frosted" | "solid"'`), and correct code examples for both values. No playground edit is needed.

The real problem is the **enforcement infrastructure** — not this specific instance.

## Root Cause

**EAP-082** — *Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page.*

The anti-pattern keeps recurring (ENG-106 was the first incident) because of a structural enforcement gap:

| Enforcer | Where it fires | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| `AGENTS.md` guardrail #24 | Doc-level only; not injected into context unless the agent explicitly reads AGENTS.md | Passive |
| `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` | `globs: playground/src/app/components/**` — fires when a playground file is opened | **Never fires when the agent only opens `Navigation.tsx`** |

The agent opens `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.tsx`, adds the `solid` branch to the class-join and the type union, closes the file, and responds — and neither enforcer was ever loaded. The obligation only existed at the destination (playground), not at the origin (component source).

## Fix

### 1. New file: `.cursor/rules/ds-components.md`

This is the missing piece. A Cursor rule glob-scoped to `src/components/ui/**` fires the parity gate **at the component-authoring site** — the exact moment the agent has the component open and is about to add a variant.

```markdown
---
globs: src/components/ui/**
---

# DS Component Authoring — Hard Guardrails

**You are editing a design-system component in `src/components/ui/`. A playground
parity update is a mandatory part of this change.**

## Playground Parity Gate (BLOCKING — runs after EVERY variant/prop/state change)

Adding or modifying a **variant**, **prop**, **appearance**, **size**, **state**, or
any visual modifier on a component in `src/components/ui/` requires a simultaneous
update to its playground page (`playground/src/app/components/<slug>/page.tsx`).
This is not deferred. It is part of the same atomic commit.

**Before responding, verify all three checks pass:**

| # | Check | Pass condition |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **ComponentPreview coverage** | One `<ComponentPreview>` block exists per value in every variant union. If `appearance = "frosted" \| "solid"`, the page must have two blocks. If you just added `"solid"`, add its block now. |
| 2 | **PropsTable accuracy** | The `type` column in `<PropsTable>` exactly mirrors the TypeScript union in the source file. If you widened the union, update the cell. |
| 3 | **Code example correctness** | Every `code={...}` snippet in `<ComponentPreview>` compiles against the current prop signature. If a prop was renamed or added, update the snippet. |

If any check fails → fix it before responding. Then apply the **Flush-and-Restart
gate** from `.cursor/rules/playground-components.md` before marking the task done.

## Anti-Pattern Reference

Violating this gate = **EAP-082** (Adding a Variant/Prop Without Updating the
Playground Page). Enforced by **AGENTS.md guardrail #24**.
Incident history: ENG-106 (first occurrence), ENG-267 (Navigation `solid` variant —
second occurrence, prompt for this rule file).

## Why this rule lives here, not only in playground-components.md

`playground-components.md` fires when the agent opens a *playground* file.
EAP-082 recurs because the agent opens `Navigation.tsx`, makes the variant change,
and never touches the playground file — so `playground-components.md` never loads.
This rule fires on the **component side of the same transaction**, closing that gap.
```

---

### 2. `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md` — prepend ENG-267

Update the `Last updated:` line:

```
**Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (ENG-267: Navigation `solid` variant added without
playground documentation — EAP-082 second recurrence. Playground page was already
correct in this session (both `frosted` and `solid` `<ComponentPreview>` blocks,
accurate `PropsTable` row, correct code examples). Root cause of the pattern:
EAP-082 enforcement existed only as (a) AGENTS.md guardrail #24 (doc-level, not
injected into context when only a component file is open) and (b)
`playground-components.md` (glob `playground/src/app/components/**`, fires only
when a playground file is opened) — neither fires when the agent touches only
`src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.tsx`. Fix: created
`.cursor/rules/ds-components.md` glob-scoped to `src/components/ui/**`; Cursor
injects it whenever any DS component source file is opened, making the 3-check
Playground Parity Gate a blocking pre-condition at the component-authoring site.
EAP-082 updated to reference new rule file. Prior: ENG-266: ...
```

---

### 3. `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` — update `Last updated:` to note EAP-082 revision

```
**Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (EAP-082 updated: enforcement gap closed — the
existing guardrail relied on AGENTS.md #24 (doc-level) and
`playground-components.md` (fires only when a playground file is already open).
ENG-267 confirmed the gap: agent edits `src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.tsx`,
adds `solid` variant, never opens the playground page, `playground-components.md`
never triggers. Fix: `.cursor/rules/ds-components.md` created, glob-scoped to
`src/components/ui/**`, with a blocking 3-check Playground Parity Gate
(ComponentPreview coverage · PropsTable accuracy · code example correctness).
ENG-267. Prior: EAP-123: ...
```

---

### 4. Playground page — no changes needed

The existing `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` passes all three parity checks already:

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| ComponentPreview coverage | ✅ Two blocks: `<Navigation />` (frosted) and `<Navigation appearance="solid" />` |
| PropsTable accuracy | ✅ `type: '"frosted" \| "solid"'` matches the `NavigationAppearance` union exactly |
| Code examples | ✅ Both `codeFrosted` and `codeSolid` constants compile against the current signature |

---

## Principle

**The enforcement obligation belongs at the origin, not the destination.**

`playground-components.md` was the right rule for "you're already in the playground, don't break things." But EAP-082 fires when someone is *not* in the playground — they're in the component source. The fix is a second glob rule that intercepts at `src/components/ui/**`, making parity a pre-condition of *adding the variant*, not a separate step that gets forgotten. Two rules, two globs, one obligation — covered from both sides.
